summary: Cathy O'Neil announces her intention to join the futurism movement, motivated by concerns about oligarchy, racist policing, and the disappearance of middle-class jobs. She critiques the field as dominated by white men aged 55-65 who obsess over technology while ignoring social issues. O'Neil argues that women are underrepresented in futurism because its core visions—immortality, brain uploading, techno-utopianism—appeal primarily to those disconnected from bodily realities and blind to technology's downsides. She plans to inject social concerns into futurist discourse, arguing that current models are fatally flawed because they focus on technological variables while ignoring human factors.
key_points: The futurism field is dominated by older white men who focus on technological optimism while ignoring social issues like racism, inequality, and job displacement
Women are less drawn to futurism because its core visions (living forever, brain uploading) seem disconnected from embodied human experience
Successful people in tech suffer from an 'I win' blindspot that makes them uncritical of systems that reward them while harming others
Current futurist models are flawed because they use too few variables, focusing on technological change while excluding human and social factors
The surveillance economy benefits those creating the technology while harming those being surveilled, like truckers being monitored
money_quotes: The only way to compete with robots is to become one
Being reminded of death once a month makes it impossible to be so silly
I call this the 'I win' blindspot, whereby people who are generally rewarded in a system seem to think the system must be great
Why should the white guys have all the fun of deciding what the future might look like?
Men grow pubes at the age of 14 and nothing ever seems to change again
They focus on technological change without taking into account human beings

If you're the one creating the surveillance software or analyzing the surveillance footage, you're getting paid really well to promote 'progress.' Not so much the story for the truckers being surveilled
key_insight: Futurism's narrow focus on technological progress while ignoring social issues stems from the demographic homogeneity of its practitioners, who benefit from current systems and remain blind to their harms.
